Defines the `Payload` message structure for sending and collecting data between devices and a mobile app.

# Purpose
The file defines a protocol buffer message structure for a `Payload` in the `pebble.pipeline` package. This `Payload` message is used to encapsulate data sent and collected from devices, such as watches and mobile devices, to a central Pipeline API. The `Payload` includes fields such as `sender`, which identifies the source of the message, and `send_time_utc`, which records the time of the latest send attempt. It also includes optional fields like `send_retry_count` and `user`, which provide additional context about the message transmission and the user associated with the data. The `Payload` can contain multiple `events` and `measurement_sets`, as well as recursive `payloads`, allowing for complex data structures that efficiently store message history. The file also specifies dependencies on other protocol buffer files, such as `common.proto`, `event.proto`, and `measurements.proto`, which define additional message types used within the `Payload`.
